published.title: The Layer Beneath: Narrative Capital, Nobel History, and the Psychology of Risk
published.description: The strongest cross-cutting theme this week is that **durable advantage lives one level below the thing everyone is looking at** — below the asset, below the invention, below the track record. Jeremy Giffon (Invest Like the Best) argues capital doesn't follow fundamentals, it follows narrative, and the narrative is now priced in real time on one shared feed. Joel Mokyr (Conversations with Tyler), fresh off his Nobel, shows that the West's five-century lead came not from inventions but from a culture-institution feedback loop that made improvement itself a habit. Gio Valiante (Knowledge Project) locates the same story inside a single skull: the binding constraint on a portfolio manager is not skill but confidence, a depreciating asset almost nobody manages deliberately. And Adam Brown's general relativity lecture (Dwarkesh) is secretly a masterclass in the same move — Einstein won by taking a "coincidence" and a constraint more seriously than anyone else. Four very different thinkers, one instruction: trade the layer beneath the visible one. Sources this week: Dwarkesh Podcast — Adam Brown – A deep but accessible introduction to general relativity; The Knowledge Project — The Mindset That Unlocks Your Full Potential | Dr. Gio Valiante; Invest Like the Best — Jeremy Giffon - The Billion Dollar PDF - [Invest Like the Best, EP.481]; Conversations with Tyler — Joel Mokyr on Clans, Corporations, and a Culture of Growth
